what: Comparing Methods Using a Shared Cross-Validation Split with tune_imp()
title: slideimp
text: Let’s use included khanmiss1 dataset. Instead random NA sampling inside tune_imp(), internally calls sample_na_loc(), can generate NA locations front pass tune_imp(). na_loc 5 elements (5 repeats) row (row col index) position missing value. can compare 1) PCA, 2) KNN imputation, 3) custom method, since cross-validation missing values methods. Note: custom function requires first argument obj, must return object dimensions, subsequent arguments must match column names parameters data.frame. PCA imputation performed best .
code: data(khanmiss1) obj <- t(khanmiss1) obj[1:3, 1:3] #>           g1   g2   g3 #> sample1 1873 1251  314 #> sample2   57 1350 1758 #> sample3   53 1140  162 na_loc <- sample_na_loc(obj, n_cols = 200, n_rows = 5, n_reps = 5) na_loc[1:3] |> lapply(head) #> [[1]] #>      row col #> [1,]  61 783 #> [2,]  21 783 #> [3,]  55 783 #> [4,]  17 783 #> [5,]  19 783 #> [6,]  40 473 #>  #> [[2]] #>      row  col #> [1,]  46  513 #> [2,]  60  513 #> [3,]  22  513 #> [4,]   7  513 #> [5,]  57  513 #> [6,]  34 1979 #>  #> [[3]] #>      row  col #> [1,]   5  276 #> [2,]  35  276 #> [3,]  31  276 #> [4,]  46  276 #> [5,]  18  276 #> [6,]  62 1012 # This custom function imputes missing values with random normal values and takes mean and sd as params rnorm_imp <- function(obj, mean, sd) {   na <- is.na(obj)   obj[na] <- rnorm(sum(na), mean = mean, sd = sd) # <- impute values with rnorm values   return(obj) # <- return an imputed object with the same dim as obj }  pca_tune <- tune_imp(   obj,   .f = "pca_imp",   na_loc = na_loc,   parameters = data.frame(ncp = 10) ) #> Tuning pca_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning  knn_tune <- tune_imp(   obj,   .f = "knn_imp",   na_loc = na_loc,   parameters = data.frame(k = 10) ) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning  rnorm_tune <- tune_imp(   obj,   .f = rnorm_imp,   na_loc = na_loc,   parameters = data.frame(mean = 0, sd = 1) # must match with arguments of `rnorm_imp` ) #> Tuning custom function #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning mean(compute_metrics(pca_tune, metrics = "rmse")$.estimate) #> [1] 454.2612 mean(compute_metrics(knn_tune, metrics = "rmse")$.estimate) #> [1] 477.9663 mean(compute_metrics(rnorm_tune, metrics = "rmse")$.estimate) #> [1] 1215.746

what: Group-Wise Imputation with Small-Group Padding and Group-Wise Parameters with group_imp()
title: slideimp
text: group_imp() allows imputation performed separately within defined groups (e.g., chromosome), significantly reduces runtime can increase accuracy K-NN PCA imputation. features: required - list-column element character vector variable names imputed together. aux: optional - auxiliary variables include group. used augment imputation quality features imputed . one group small (e.g., chrM), aux used pad group randomly drawing samples groups meet min_group_size. parameters: optional - group-specific imputation parameters. First simulate data 2 groups. create group3 1 feature show min_group_size pads using aux list column. generate group parameter group_imp() front. can see group3 padded 10 columns. group, also assign different number nearest neighbors demonstration. Finally, impute obj using modified group_imp_df. k = 10 passed group_imp() ignored since groups group-wise k specified.
code: sim_obj <- sim_mat(n = 20, p = 50, n_col_groups = 2)  # Matrix to be imputed obj <- sim_obj$input obj[1:5, 1:4] #>           feature1   feature2  feature3  feature4 #> sample1 1.00000000 0.83993889 0.8425299 1.0000000 #> sample2 0.04193941 0.03671375 0.3732779        NA #> sample3 0.70167887 1.00000000        NA 0.8740183 #> sample4 0.22303103 0.05777196 0.3740496 0.5036475 #> sample5 0.40880574 0.80651233 1.0000000 0.6259976  # Metadata, i.e., which features belong to which group meta <- sim_obj$col_group meta[1:5, ] #>    feature  group #> 1 feature1 group1 #> 2 feature2 group2 #> 3 feature3 group2 #> 4 feature4 group1 #> 5 feature5 group2  # We put feature 1 in `group3` meta[1, 2] <- "group3" meta[1:5, ] #>    feature  group #> 1 feature1 group3 #> 2 feature2 group2 #> 3 feature3 group2 #> 4 feature4 group1 #> 5 feature5 group2 set.seed(1234) group_imp_df <- prep_groups(colnames(obj), group = meta, min_group_size = 10) group_imp_df$parameters <- list(list(k = 3), list(k = 4), list(k = 5)) group_imp_df #> # slideimp table: 3 x 4 #>   group          feature             aux parameters #>  group1 <character [24]> <character [0]> <list [1]> #>  group2 <character [25]> <character [0]> <list [1]> #>  group3  <character [1]> <character [9]> <list [1]> knn_results <- group_imp(obj, group = group_imp_df, cores = 4, k = 10) #> Imputing 3 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: parallel (OpenMP within groups)... print(knn_results, p = 4) #> slideimp_results (KNN) #> Dimensions: 20 x 50 #>  #>           feature1   feature2  feature3   feature4 #> sample1 1.00000000 0.83993889 0.8425299 1.00000000 #> sample2 0.04193941 0.03671375 0.3732779 0.02400081 #> sample3 0.70167887 1.00000000 0.9305440 0.87401826 #> sample4 0.22303103 0.05777196 0.3740496 0.50364753 #> sample5 0.40880574 0.80651233 1.0000000 0.62599759 #> sample6 0.37769554 0.22618483 0.5819875 0.24203100 #>  #> # Showing [1:6, 1:4] of full matrix

previous_headings: Sliding Window Imputation for WGBS/EM-seq Data with slide_imp()
what: Select window_size, overlap_size, and PCA/KNN Parameters
title: slideimp
text: Note: WGBS/EM-seq data grouped chromosome performing sliding window imputation. , simulate 1000 sites. Importantly, simulate location vector feature (genomic position) varying spacing 50 500 bp apart . locations vector contains genomic position feature (column). used determine columns grouped together given window size. First, convert data beta matrix features columns. , real dataset, tune hyperparameters using chr22. , demonstration, use whole data since size small. ncp 2 4, indicating performing sliding PCA imputation. Pass k sliding KNN imputation. window_size 5,000 10,000 bp. overlap_size fixed 1,000 bp (affect results much real analyses). Finally, can use slide_imp() impute full beta_matrix. Use best parameter combination cross-validation metrics. start end window location vectors. window_n number features included column. Turn-dry_run impute data
code: set.seed(1234) sample_names <- paste0("S", 1:10) n_sites <- 1000  # Simulate positions with 50–500 bp between each site distances_between <- sample(50:500, size = n_sites, replace = TRUE) locations <- cumsum(distances_between) # <- important, location vector  methyl <- data.frame(   chr = "chr1",   start = locations,   end = locations,   strand = "+" )  for (i in seq_along(sample_names)) {   methyl[[paste0("numCs", i)]] <- sample.int(100, size = n_sites, replace = TRUE)   methyl[[paste0("numTs", i)]] <- sample.int(100, size = n_sites, replace = TRUE)   methyl[[paste0("coverage", i)]] <- methyl[[paste0("numCs", i)]] + methyl[[paste0("numTs", i)]] }  methyl[1:5, 1:10] #>    chr start  end strand numCs1 numTs1 coverage1 numCs2 numTs2 coverage2 #> 1 chr1   333  333      +     83     68       151     84     36       120 #> 2 chr1   718  718      +     82      2        84     54     99       153 #> 3 chr1  1173 1173      +     48      2        50     44     16        60 #> 4 chr1  1323 1323      +     70     48       118     48     56       104 #> 5 chr1  1483 1483      +     88     15       103     83     19       102 numCs_matrix <- as.matrix(methyl[, paste0("numCs", seq_along(sample_names))]) cov_matrix <- as.matrix(methyl[, paste0("coverage", seq_along(sample_names))]) beta_matrix <- numCs_matrix / cov_matrix  colnames(beta_matrix) <- sample_names rownames(beta_matrix) <- methyl$start  beta_matrix <- t(beta_matrix) # Set 10% of the data to missing set.seed(1234) beta_matrix[sample.int(length(beta_matrix), floor(length(beta_matrix) * 0.1))] <- NA beta_matrix[1:4, 1:4] #>           333       718       1173      1323 #> S1 0.54966887 0.9761905 0.96000000 0.5932203 #> S2 0.70000000 0.3529412 0.73333333 0.4615385 #> S3 0.06521739 0.7053571         NA 0.6507937 #> S4 0.69444444 0.2846154 0.04347826 0.7636364 params <- expand.grid(ncp = c(2, 4), window_size = c(5000, 10000)) params$overlap_size <- 1000 params$min_window_n <- 20 # windows with less than 20 columns are dropped  # Increase n_reps from 2 in actual analyses and use another chromosome (i.e., chr22) tune_slide_pca <- tune_imp(   obj = beta_matrix,   parameters = params,   .f = "slide_imp",   n_reps = 2,   location = locations ) #> Tuning slide_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> ℹ Using default `num_na` = 500 (~5% of cells). #>   Increase for more reliability or decrease if missing is dense. #> Running Mode: sequential... #>  #> Step 2/2: Tuning  metrics <- compute_metrics(tune_slide_pca)  aggregate(.estimate ~ .metric + ncp + window_size, data = metrics, FUN = mean) #>   .metric ncp window_size .estimate #> 1     mae   2        5000 0.2257168 #> 2    rmse   2        5000 0.2838435 #> 3     mae   4        5000 0.2395280 #> 4    rmse   4        5000 0.2984013 #> 5     mae   2       10000 0.2123151 #> 6    rmse   2       10000 0.2642956 #> 7     mae   4       10000 0.2223801 #> 8    rmse   4       10000 0.2756162 slide_imp(   obj = beta_matrix,   location = locations,   window_size = 5000,   overlap_size = 1000,   ncp = 2,   min_window_n = 20,   dry_run = TRUE # <- dry_run to inspect the windows ) #> Dropping 43 window(s) with fewer than 20 columns. #> # slideimp table: 24 x 4 #>  start end window_n  subset_local #>     15  34       20 <double [20]> #>    104 124       21 <double [21]> #>    188 209       22 <double [22]> #>    205 225       21 <double [21]> #>    222 241       20 <double [20]> #>    239 258       20 <double [20]> #>    254 273       20 <double [20]> #>    369 388       20 <double [20]> #>    385 404       20 <double [20]> #>    402 424       23 <double [23]> #> # ... with 14 more rows slide_imp(   obj = beta_matrix,   location = locations,   window_size = 5000,   overlap_size = 1000,   ncp = 2,   min_window_n = 20,   dry_run = FALSE ) #> Dropping 43 window(s) with fewer than 20 columns. #> Step 1/2: Imputing #>  Processing window 1 of 24 #>  Processing window 2 of 24 #>  Processing window 3 of 24 #>  Processing window 4 of 24 #>  Processing window 5 of 24 #>  Processing window 6 of 24 #>  Processing window 7 of 24 #>  Processing window 8 of 24 #>  Processing window 9 of 24 #>  Processing window 10 of 24 #>  Processing window 11 of 24 #>  Processing window 12 of 24 #>  Processing window 13 of 24 #>  Processing window 14 of 24 #>  Processing window 15 of 24 #>  Processing window 16 of 24 #>  Processing window 17 of 24 #>  Processing window 18 of 24 #>  Processing window 19 of 24 #>  Processing window 20 of 24 #>  Processing window 21 of 24 #>  Processing window 22 of 24 #>  Processing window 23 of 24 #>  Processing window 24 of 24 #> Step 2/2: Averaging overlapping regions #> Note: 551 column(s) not covered by any window; original values retained. #> slideimp_results (PCA) #> Dimensions: 10 x 1000 #>  #>           333       718       1173      1323      1483       1925 #> S1 0.54966887 0.9761905 0.96000000 0.5932203        NA 0.08695652 #> S2 0.70000000 0.3529412 0.73333333 0.4615385 0.8137255 0.45454545 #> S3 0.06521739 0.7053571         NA 0.6507937 0.4393064 0.37735849 #> S4 0.69444444 0.2846154 0.04347826 0.7636364 0.9595960 0.54621849 #> S5 0.83505155 0.5777778 0.47517730        NA 0.7368421 0.21666667 #> S6 0.26612903 0.3451327 0.53072626 0.5000000 0.6465517 0.37288136 #>  #> # title: Numeric Matrices K-NN and PCA Imputation
text: simulated beta matrix MSA microarray. CpGs columns. Chromosome-wise imputation Illumina microarrays can performed single function call (requires Illumina manifests, conveniently provided slideimp.extra package). example demonstrates PCA imputation. use KNN imputation instead, supply k argument. Remove “ctl” probes (control probes) imputation obj <- obj[, !grepl("^ctl", colnames(obj))] set allow_unmapped = TRUE ignore . Deduplicate MSA EPICv2 data imputation slideimp.extra::dedup_matrix(). Speed KNN imputation OpenMP using cores argument instead mirai (available default Windows Linux). need clock CpGs, provide subset argument ignore probes.
code: dim(MSA_beta_matrix) # [1]     20 281797 MSA_beta_matrix[1:4, 1:4] #         cg06185909_TC11 cg18975462_BC11 cg20516119_TC11 cg10149399_BC11 # sample1              NA       0.5023435       0.3835431              NA # sample2       0.4907466       0.5095459       0.9025816       0.4313347 # sample3       0.6885036              NA       0.7646753       0.4498772 # sample4       0.0000000       0.0000000              NA       0.0000000 library(slideimp) library(slideimp.extra) library(mirai)  imputed <- group_imp(   obj = MSA_beta_matrix,   group = "MSA", # <- this feature requires the `slideimp.extra` package   ncp = 10, # <- change to `k` for KNN imputation   .progress = FALSE # <- turn on to monitor progress of longer running jobs ) # Found cleaned manifest for 'MSA' # Groups 24 dropped: no features remaining after matching obj columns. # Imputing 25 group(s) using PCA. # Running Mode: sequential ...  print(imputed, n = 4, p = 4) # slideimp_results (PCA) # Dimensions: 20 x 281797 #  #         cg06185909_TC11 cg18975462_BC11 cg20516119_TC11 cg10149399_BC11 # sample1       0.1517542       0.5023435      0.38354308       0.2067731 # sample2       0.4907466       0.5095459      0.90258164       0.4313347 # sample3       0.6885036       0.7339375      0.76467530       0.4498772 # sample4       0.0000000       0.0000000      0.05230101       0.0000000 #  # # Showing [1:4, 1:4] of full matrix

title: Numeric Matrices K-NN and PCA Imputation
text: simulate DNA methylation (DNAm) microarray data 2 chromosomes. slideimp functions expect input numeric matrix variables stored columns. Hyperparameters tuned using tune_imp(). evaluate following options grid search: Number nearest neighbors (k): 5 20 Inverse distance power (dist_pow) weighted averaging: 1 2 (higher values assign lower weights distant neighbors) Tuning performed subset data. use 10 repeats (n_reps = 10) cross-validation evaluation. re-impute 50 observed values (num_na = 50) compute rmse mae. Increase n_reps num_na real analyses increase reliability error estimates. Note: Parallelization via cores argument available KNN imputation OpenMP. Calculate errors using compute_metrics() {yardsticks} functions. K-NN without OpenMP, PCA imputation, custom functions (see vignette), set parallelization mirai::daemons(). Note: machines multi-threaded BLAS, turn pin_blas = TRUE tuning PCA imputation parallel avoid thrashing. perform imputation group_imp() using best parameters. PCA imputation group_imp() can parallelized using mirai package, similar tune_imp(). pin_blas = TRUE can help PCA imputation multi-threaded BLAS machines. Alternatively, full matrix imputation can performed using knn_imp() pca_imp().
code: library(slideimp) set.seed(1234) sim_obj <- sim_mat(n = 20, p = 100, n_col_groups = 2)  # Extract the $input attribute obj <- sim_obj$input  # Extract metadata to map the features to groups group_df <- sim_obj$col_group knn_params <- expand.grid(k = c(5, 20), dist_pow = c(1, 2)) group2_columns <- subset(group_df, group == "group2") group2_only <- obj[, group2_columns$feature]  tune_knn <- tune_imp(   group2_only,   parameters = knn_params,   .f = "knn_imp",   cores = 8,   n_reps = 10,   num_na = 50 ) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: parallel... #> Step 2/2: Tuning metrics <- compute_metrics(tune_knn)  # equivalently: dplyr::summarize(metrics, ..., .by = c(k, dist_pow, .metric)) sum_metrics <- do.call(   data.frame,   aggregate(     .estimate ~ k + dist_pow + .metric,     data = metrics,     FUN = function(x) {       c(         n = length(x),         mean_error = mean(x),         sd_error = sd(x)       )     }   ) )  sum_metrics[order(sum_metrics$.estimate.mean_error), ] #>    k dist_pow .metric .estimate.n .estimate.mean_error .estimate.sd_error #> 2 20        1     mae          10            0.1501427         0.01599598 #> 4 20        2     mae          10            0.1519464         0.01638402 #> 1  5        1     mae          10            0.1656087         0.01683512 #> 3  5        2     mae          10            0.1669792         0.01666404 #> 6 20        1    rmse          10            0.1897722         0.01915653 #> 8 20        2    rmse          10            0.1918765         0.01936392 #> 5  5        1    rmse          10            0.2081833         0.01949624 #> 7  5        2    rmse          10            0.2101955         0.01884442 mirai::daemons(2) # 2 Cores  # PCA imputation. pca_params <- data.frame(ncp = c(1, 5)) # For machines multi-threaded BLAS, turn on `pin_blas = TRUE` tune_pca <- tune_imp(obj, parameters = pca_params, .f = "pca_imp", n_reps = 10, num_na = 50)  mirai::daemons(0) # Close daemons knn_group_results <- group_imp(obj, group = group_df, k = 20, dist_pow = 1, cores = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: parallel (OpenMP within groups)... knn_group_results #> slideimp_results (KNN) #> Dimensions: 20 x 100 #>  #>          feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4  feature5  feature6 #> sample1 0.3486482 0.7385414 0.4077444 0.1607935 0.3924661 0.2434143 #> sample2 0.5338935 0.4724364 0.9663621 0.4788070 0.5061132 0.3923603 #> sample3 0.7185848 0.7351035 0.6724479 0.3162537 0.7634236 1.0000000 #> sample4 0.1734418 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.2106358 #> sample5 0.5388440 0.5306182 0.5685354 0.5383513 0.4680080 0.8518388 #> sample6 0.3768380 0.5570723 0.8764909 0.5276245 0.6722794 0.5740639 #>  #> # Showing [1:6, 1:6] of full matrix # Similar to `tune_imp`, parallelization is controlled by `mirai::daemons()` mirai::daemons(2) pca_group_results <- group_imp(obj, group = group_df, ncp = 10) mirai::daemons(0) full_knn_results <- knn_imp(obj = obj, k = 20) full_pca_results <- pca_imp(obj = obj, ncp = 10)

title: Numeric Matrices K-NN and PCA Imputation
text: slide_imp() performs sliding window imputation. Note: DNAm WGBS/EM-seq data grouped chromosomes (.e., run slide_imp() separately chromosome) imputation. See package vignette details. function Illumina DNAm microarrays; see group_imp(). See vignette details selecting window_size overlap_size tune_imp(). slide_imp() parameters: location: required - sorted numeric vector length ncol(obj) giving position column (e.g., genomic coordinates bp). window_size: required - width sliding window (unit location). overlap_size: optional - overlap width consecutive windows (units location). Must strictly less window_size. min_window_n: required - minimum number columns window must contain imputed. Windows smaller dropped. Must greater k (KNN) ncp (PCA). dry_run: optional - return just calculated windows examine windows included. k: required - (specifying KNN imputation) number nearest neighbors use inside window. ncp: required - (specifying PCA imputation) number principal components retain. Use instead k performing sliding-window PCA imputation. subset: optional - impute just subset features (.e., just clock CpGs). flank: optional - build flanking windows window_size around features provided subset. First, let’s perform dry run examine windows imputed slide_imp. , can perform imputation using given parameters.

title: Numeric Matrices K-NN and PCA Imputation
text: Choices "EPICv2" "MSA" (requires {slideimp.extra} package). (Basic): data.frame containing feature column (character strings feature names) group column (defining group). (Advanced): use prep_groups() create groups based mapping data.frame (.e., Illumina manifests) custom group-wise parameters. slide_imp(): Sliding window K-NN PCA imputation extremely high-dimensional numeric matrices (WGBS/EM-seq) ordered features (.e., genomic position). tune_imp(): Parallelizable hyperparameter tuning repeated cross-validation; works built-custom imputation functions. knn_imp(): Full-matrix K-NN imputation multi-core parallelization, {mlpack} KD/Ball-Tree nearest neighbor implementation (data low missing rates extremely high dimensions), optional subset imputation (ideal epigenetic clock calculations). pca_imp(): Optimized version missMDA::imputePCA() high-dimensional numeric matrices.

title: Grouped K-NN or PCA Imputation — group_imp
text: obj numeric matrix samples rows features columns. group Specification features grouped imputation. Accepts three formats: character: (Requires {slideimp.extra} package, available GitHub). single string naming supported Illumina platform (e.g., "EPICv2", "EPICv2_deduped"). manifest fetched automatically. installation instructions, see package README. Supported platforms can viewed via slideimp.extra::slideimp_arrays. data.frame (Long format): group: Column identifying group feature. feature: Character column individual feature names. data.frame (List-column format): feature: List-column character vectors impute (e.g., prep_groups()). aux: (Optional) List-column auxiliary names used context. parameters: (Optional) List-column group-specific parameter lists. subset Character vector feature names impute (default NULL means impute features). Must subset obj_cn (colnames(obj)) must appear least one group's feature. Features group subset demoted auxiliary columns group. Groups left zero features demotion dropped message. allow_unmapped Logical. FALSE, every column colnames(obj) must appear group. TRUE, columns group assignment left untouched (neither imputed used auxiliary columns) message issued instead error. k Number nearest neighbors imputation. 10 good starting point. ncp Integer. Number components used predict missing entries. method K-NN imputation: distance metric use ("euclidean" "manhattan"). PCA imputation: regularization imputation algorithm ("regularized" "EM"). cores number OpenMP cores K-NN imputation . PCA mirai-based parallelism, use mirai::daemons() instead. .progress Show imputation progress (default = TRUE). min_group_size Integer NULL. Minimum number columns (features + aux) per group. Groups smaller padded randomly sampled columns obj. Passed prep_groups() internally. colmax number 0 1. Threshold column-wise missing data rate K-NN imputation skipped. post_imp Whether impute remaining missing values (failed K-NN imputation) using column means (default = TRUE). dist_pow amount penalization away nearest neighbors weighted average. dist_pow = 0 (default) simple average nearest neighbors. tree Logical. FALSE (default) = brute-force K-NN. TRUE = use {mlpack} BallTree. max_cache Maximum allowed cache size GB (default 4). greater 0, pairwise distances columns missing values pre-computed cached, faster moderate-sized data uses O(m^2) memory m number columns missing values. Set 0 disable caching trade speed lower memory usage. scale Logical. TRUE (default), variables scaled unit variance. coeff.ridge Numeric. Ridge regularization coefficient (default 1). used method = "regularized". Values < 1 regularize less (closer EM); values > 1 regularize (closer mean imputation). threshold Numeric. threshold assessing convergence. row.w Row weights (internally normalized sum 1). Can one : NULL (default): rows weighted equally. numeric vector: Custom positive weights length nrow(obj). "n_miss": Rows missing values receive lower weight. seed Numeric NULL. Random seed reproducibility padding min_group_size passed pca_imp(). nb.init Integer. Number random initializations. first initialization always mean imputation. maxiter Integer. Maximum number iterations algorithm. miniter Integer. Minimum number iterations algorithm. pin_blas Logical. TRUE, pin BLAS thread 1 help parallel performance systems linked multi-threaded BLAS.

title: Grouped K-NN or PCA Imputation — group_imp
text: Parallelization behavior depends imputation method: KNN: use cores argument (OpenMP available). mirai::daemons() also active, cores automatically set 1 avoid nested parallelism. PCA: use mirai::daemons() instead cores. Linux / OpenBLAS / MKL users: machine uses multi-threaded BLAS (e.g., OpenBLAS Intel MKL), set pin_blas = TRUE tuning PCA imputation parallel. Without , BLAS threads mirai workers compete cores, can cause slowdowns (CPU thrashing). macOS users: OpenMP typically unavailable macOS unless manually configured. cores fall back 1 automatically; use mirai::daemons() parallelization instead.

code: # Generate example data with missing values set.seed(1234) to_test <- sim_mat(50, 20, perc_total_na = 0.3, perc_col_na = 1) obj <- to_test$input group <- to_test$col_group # metadata that maps `colnames(obj)` to groups head(group) #>    feature  group #> 1 feature1 group2 #> 2 feature2 group2 #> 3 feature3 group2 #> 4 feature4 group2 #> 5 feature5 group2 #> 6 feature6 group2  # Simple grouped K-NN imputation results <- group_imp(obj, group = group, k = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: sequential ...  # Impute only a subset of features subset_features <- sample(to_test$col_group$feature, size = 10) knn_subset <- group_imp(obj, group = group, subset = subset_features, k = 5) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: sequential ...  # Use prep_groups() to inspect and tweak per-group parameters prepped <- prep_groups(colnames(obj), group) prepped$parameters <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(prepped)), \(i) list(k = 5)) prepped$parameters[[2]]$k <- 10 knn_grouped <- group_imp(obj, group = prepped, cores = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using KNN. #> Running Mode: parallel (OpenMP within groups)...  # PCA imputation with mirai parallelism mirai::daemons(2) pca_grouped <- group_imp(obj, group = group, ncp = 2) #> Imputing 2 group(s) using PCA. #> Running Mode: parallel (mirai across groups)... #> Tip: set `pin_blas = TRUE` may improve parallel performance. mirai::daemons(0) pca_grouped #> slideimp_results (PCA) #> Dimensions: 50 x 20 #>  #>           feature1   feature2  feature3  feature4  feature5  feature6 #> sample1 0.05685662 0.39194101 0.2995808 0.5266989 0.3693625 0.3372606 #> sample2 0.49992298 0.54447261 0.5504182 0.6699885 0.7143775 0.7084667 #> sample3 0.54575968 0.78179817 0.8510282 0.7503179 0.7469653 0.8390176 #> sample4 0.00000000 0.07766851 0.2063256 0.2967793 0.2200145 0.2558501 #> sample5 0.59340652 0.50932703 0.5127284 0.6909102 0.5803437 0.7547226 #> sample6 0.62356150 0.64673748 0.5254893 0.7499320 0.6118071 0.6436882 #>  #> # Accepts three formats: character: (Requires {slideimp.extra} package, available GitHub). single string naming supported Illumina platform (e.g., "EPICv2", "EPICv2_deduped"). manifest fetched automatically. installation instructions, see package README. Supported platforms can viewed via slideimp.extra::slideimp_arrays. data.frame (Long format): group: Column identifying group feature. feature: Character column individual feature names. data.frame (List-column format): feature: List-column character vectors impute (e.g., prep_groups()). aux: (Optional) List-column auxiliary names used context. parameters: (Optional) List-column group-specific parameter lists. subset Character vector feature names impute (default NULL means impute features). Must subset obj_cn (colnames(obj)) must appear least one group's feature. Features group subset demoted auxiliary columns group. Groups left zero features demotion dropped message. min_group_size Integer NULL. Minimum number columns (features + aux) per group. Groups smaller padded randomly sampled columns obj. Passed prep_groups() internally. allow_unmapped Logical. FALSE, every column colnames(obj) must appear group. TRUE, columns group assignment left untouched (neither imputed used auxiliary columns) message issued instead error. seed Numeric NULL. Random seed reproducibility padding min_group_size passed pca_imp().

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
code: slide_imp(   obj,   location,   window_size,   overlap_size = 0,   flank = FALSE,   min_window_n,   subset = NULL,   dry_run = FALSE,   k = NULL,   colmax = 0.9,   cores = 1,   post_imp = FALSE,   dist_pow = 0,   max_cache = 4,   ncp = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   coeff.ridge = 1,   seed = NULL,   row.w = NULL,   nb.init = 1,   maxiter = 1000,   miniter = 5,   method = NULL,   .progress = TRUE )

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
text: obj numeric matrix samples rows features columns. location sorted numeric vector length ncol(obj) giving position column (e.g., genomic coordinates). Used define sliding windows. window_size Window width units location. overlap_size Overlap consecutive windows units location. Must less window_size. Default 0. Ignored flank = TRUE. flank Logical. TRUE, instead sliding windows across whole matrix, one window width window_size created flanking feature listed subset. mode overlap_size ignored. Requires subset provided. Default = FALSE. min_window_n Minimum number columns window must contain imputed. Windows smaller imputed. k ncp must also smaller min_window_n. subset Character vector column names integer vector column indices specifying columns impute. dry_run Logical. TRUE, skip imputation return slideimp_tbl object windows used (dropping rules). k/ncp required mode. Columns: start, end, window_n, plus subset_local (list-column local subset indices) flank = FALSE, target subset_local flank = TRUE. Default = FALSE. k Number nearest neighbors imputation. 10 good starting point. colmax number 0 1. Threshold column-wise missing data rate K-NN imputation skipped. cores Number cores KNN parallelization (OpenMP). macOS, OpenMP may need additional compiler configuration. post_imp Whether impute remaining missing values (failed K-NN imputation) using column means (default = TRUE). dist_pow amount penalization away nearest neighbors weighted average. dist_pow = 0 (default) simple average nearest neighbors. max_cache Maximum allowed cache size GB (default 4). greater 0, pairwise distances columns missing values pre-computed cached, faster moderate-sized data uses O(m^2) memory m number columns missing values. Set 0 disable caching trade speed lower memory usage. ncp Integer. Number components used predict missing entries. scale Logical. TRUE (default), variables scaled unit variance. coeff.ridge Numeric. Ridge regularization coefficient (default 1). used method = "regularized". Values < 1 regularize less (closer EM); values > 1 regularize (closer mean imputation). seed Integer. Random number generator seed. row.w Row weights (internally normalized sum 1). Can one : NULL (default): rows weighted equally. numeric vector: Custom positive weights length nrow(obj). "n_miss": Rows missing values receive lower weight. nb.init Integer. Number random initializations. first initialization always mean imputation. maxiter Integer. Maximum number iterations algorithm. miniter Integer. Minimum number iterations algorithm. method K-NN imputation: distance metric use ("euclidean" "manhattan"). PCA imputation: regularization imputation algorithm ("regularized" "EM"). .progress Show progress bar (default = TRUE).

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
text: numeric matrix dimensions obj missing values imputed. dry_run = TRUE, returns data.frame class slideimp_tbl columns start, end, window_n, plus subset_local (target flank = TRUE).

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
text: sliding window approach divides input matrix smaller segments based location values applies imputation window independently. Values overlapping areas averaged across windows produce final imputed result. Two windowing modes supported: flank = FALSE (default): Greedily partitions location vector windows width window_size requested overlap_size consecutive windows. flank = TRUE: Creates one window per feature subset exactly flanks specific feature using supplied window_size. Specify k related arguments use knn_imp(), ncp related arguments pca_imp().

title: Sliding Window K-NN or PCA Imputation — slide_imp
code: # Generate sample data with missing values with 20 samples and 100 columns # where the column order is sorted (i.e., by genomic position) set.seed(1234) beta_matrix <- sim_mat(20, 100)$input location <- 1:100  # It's very useful to first perform a dry run to examine the calculated windows windows_statistics <- slide_imp(   beta_matrix,   location = location,   window_size = 50,   overlap_size = 10,   min_window_n = 10,   dry_run = TRUE ) windows_statistics #> # slideimp table: 3 x 4 #>  start end window_n  subset_local #>      1  50       50 <double [50]> #>     41  90       50 <double [50]> #>     81 100       20 <double [20]>  # Sliding Window K-NN imputation by specifying `k` (sliding windows) imputed_knn <- slide_imp(   beta_matrix,   location = location,   k = 5,   window_size = 50,   overlap_size = 10,   min_window_n = 10,   scale = FALSE # This argument belongs to PCA imputation and will be ignored ) #> Step 1/2: Imputing #>  Processing window 1 of 3 #>  Processing window 2 of 3 #>  Processing window 3 of 3 #> Step 2/2: Averaging overlapping regions imputed_knn #> slideimp_results (KNN) #> Dimensions: 20 x 100 #>  #>          feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4  feature5  feature6 #> sample1 0.3486482 0.7385414 0.4077444 0.1607935 0.3924661 0.2434143 #> sample2 0.5338935 0.4724364 0.9663621 0.4922511 0.5061132 0.3923603 #> sample3 0.7185848 0.7351035 0.6724479 0.3162537 0.7634236 1.0000000 #> sample4 0.1734418 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.2106358 #> sample5 0.4499620 0.5306182 0.5685354 0.5383513 0.4680080 0.8518388 #> sample6 0.3768380 0.5570723 0.8764909 0.5276245 0.6722794 0.5740639 #>  #> # Showing [1:6, 1:6] of full matrix  # Sliding Window PCA imputation by specifying `ncp` (sliding windows) pca_knn <- slide_imp(   beta_matrix,   location = location,   ncp = 2,   window_size = 50,   overlap_size = 10,   min_window_n = 10 ) #> Step 1/2: Imputing #>  Processing window 1 of 3 #>  Processing window 2 of 3 #>  Processing window 3 of 3 #> Step 2/2: Averaging overlapping regions pca_knn #> slideimp_results (PCA) #> Dimensions: 20 x 100 #>  #>          feature1  feature2  feature3  feature4  feature5  feature6 #> sample1 0.3486482 0.7385414 0.4077444 0.1607935 0.3924661 0.2434143 #> sample2 0.5338935 0.4724364 0.9663621 0.4242777 0.5061132 0.3923603 #> sample3 0.7185848 0.7351035 0.6724479 0.3162537 0.7634236 1.0000000 #> sample4 0.1734418 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.2106358 #> sample5 0.4971181 0.5306182 0.5685354 0.5383513 0.4680080 0.8518388 #> sample6 0.3768380 0.5570723 0.8764909 0.5276245 0.6722794 0.5740639 #>  #> # title: Tune Parameters for Imputation Methods — tune_imp
text: data.frame class c("slideimp_tune", "slideimp_tbl", "data.frame") containing following columns: ...: columns originally provided parameters. param_set: integer ID representing unique parameter combination. rep_id: integer indicating repetition index. result: nested list-column element data.frame containing truth (original values) estimate (imputed values). error: character column containing error message iteration failed, otherwise NA.

title: Tune Parameters for Imputation Methods — tune_imp
text: function supports tuning built-imputation methods ("slide_imp", "knn_imp", "pca_imp") custom functions provided via .f. .f character string, columns parameters validated chosen method's requirements: "knn_imp": requires k parameters "pca_imp": requires ncp parameters "slide_imp": requires window_size, overlap_size, min_window_n, plus exactly one k ncp .f custom function, columns parameters must correspond arguments .f (excluding obj argument). custom function must accept obj (numeric matrix) first argument return numeric matrix identical dimensions. Tuning results can evaluated using yardstick package compute_metrics().

title: Tune Parameters for Imputation Methods — tune_imp
text: Parallelization behavior depends imputation method: KNN: use cores argument (OpenMP available). mirai::daemons() also active, cores automatically set 1 avoid nested parallelism. PCA: use mirai::daemons() instead cores. Linux / OpenBLAS / MKL users: machine uses multi-threaded BLAS (e.g., OpenBLAS Intel MKL), set pin_blas = TRUE tuning PCA imputation parallel. Without , BLAS threads mirai workers compete cores, can cause slowdowns (CPU thrashing). macOS users: OpenMP typically unavailable macOS unless manually configured. cores fall back 1 automatically; use mirai::daemons() parallelization instead.

title: Tune Parameters for Imputation Methods — tune_imp
code: # Setup example data data(khanmiss1) obj <- t(khanmiss1)[1:20, sample.int(nrow(khanmiss1), size = 200)]  # 1. Tune K-NN imputation with random NA injection params_knn <- data.frame(k = c(5, 10)) results <- tune_imp(obj, params_knn, .f = "knn_imp", n_reps = 1, num_na = 20) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning compute_metrics(results) #>    k param_set rep_id error  n n_miss .metric .estimator .estimate #> 1  5         1      1  <NA> 20      0     mae   standard  463.8675 #> 2  5         1      1  <NA> 20      0    rmse   standard  634.3499 #> 3 10         2      1  <NA> 20      0     mae   standard  485.9833 #> 4 10         2      1  <NA> 20      0    rmse   standard  625.0286  # 2. Tune with fixed NA positions na_positions <- list(   matrix(c(1, 2, 3, 1, 1, 1), ncol = 2),   matrix(c(2, 3, 4, 2, 2, 2), ncol = 2) ) results_fixed <- tune_imp(   obj,   data.frame(k = 10),   .f = "knn_imp",   na_loc = na_positions ) #> Tuning knn_imp #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning  # 3. Custom imputation function custom_fill <- function(obj, val = 0) {   obj[is.na(obj)] <- val   obj } tune_imp(obj, data.frame(val = c(0, 1)), .f = custom_fill, num_na = 10) #> Tuning custom function #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: sequential... #> Step 2/2: Tuning #> # slideimp table: 2 x 5 #>  val param_set rep_id        result error #>    0         1      1 <df [10 x 2]>  <NA> #>    1         2      1 <df [10 x 2]>  <NA>  # 4. Parallel tuning (requires mirai package) mirai::daemons(2) parameters_custom <- data.frame(mean = c(0, 1), sd = c(1, 1))  # Define a simple custom function for illustration custom_imp <- function(obj, mean, sd) {   na_pos <- is.na(obj)   obj[na_pos] <- stats::rnorm(sum(na_pos), mean = mean, sd = sd)   obj }  results_p <- tune_imp(obj, parameters_custom, .f = custom_imp, n_reps = 1, num_na = 10) #> Tuning custom function #> Step 1/2: Resolving NA locations #> Running Mode: parallel... #> Step 2/2: Tuning #> Tip: set `pin_blas = TRUE` may improve parallel performance. mirai::daemons(0) # Close workers

title: slideimp 1.0.0
text: group_features() renamed prep_groups() better reflect purpose. now accepts column name vector instead full matrix. group_imp() enforces stricter data validation. requested feature subset must subset object’s column names mapping data frame. Set allow_unmapped = TRUE bypass errors intersections incomplete. group_imp() tune_imp() now error arguments supplied apply chosen imputation method, rather silently ignoring . inject_na() renamed sample_na_loc() now exported. original remains accessible via slideimp:::inject_na() legacy code. knn_imp() uses logical tree argument toggle Ball tree (TRUE) brute force (FALSE). KD tree longer supported. sim_mat() returns matrix sample--column format immediate compatibility package functions. perc_NA renamed perc_total_na, dimensions now specified via n (rows) p (columns). tune_imp() gains unified method argument applies pca_imp() knn_imp(), replacing pca_method knn_method. rep argument renamed n_reps. tune_imp() results v0.5.4 longer reproducible internal NA generation now uses sample_na_loc().

title: slideimp 1.0.0
text: compute_metrics() supports data frames result list column containing truth estimate columns, similar yardstick. knn_imp() gains max_cache control internal cache size (defaults 4GB). sim_mat() gains rho argument support compound symmetry correlation structures simulated matrices. sim_mat() tune_imp() gain dedicated print methods provide concise summaries instead dumping raw data console. slide_imp() gains location, flank, dry_run arguments fixed-window imputation, “flank mode” features surrounding subset, pre-computation inspection window statistics. tune_imp() gains granular control NA injection via n_cols, n_rows, num_na, na_col_subset. Pre-calculated locations can also passed na_loc compare methods using identical NA patterns.

title: slideimp 1.0.0
text: Dependencies streamlined. tibble purrr removed hard dependencies, cli added informative messaging, carrier added explicit dependency. Documentation thoroughly overhauled numerous consistency improvements bug fixes. RhpcBLASctl added suggested package allow pinning BLAS cores avoid thrashing parallel runs. {slideimp.extra} added suggested package, available via R-universe. provides lightweight Illumina manifests frictionless group_imp() calls. group_imp() tune_imp() prioritize process-level parallelization via mirai. knn_imp() supports OpenMP-controlled parallelization via cores argument mirai daemons active. knn_imp() pca_imp() use optimized internal Rcpp functions better performance.
